acceptanceCriteria: Confirmed whether xyo-chain's own runtime logging captures fatal handler/startup stacks
Decision recorded: keep quiet-in-prod OR decouple (keep stack, drop only usage block)
If decoupled, reportHandlerFailure prints the raw error/stack in prod on stderr while still omitting the usage block; tests updated
description: Feature 1 (shipped ea31885, cli-kit-yargs runYargsApplication) drops the yargs usage/help block on handler-origin failures (an unambiguous win) but ALSO suppresses the raw error STACK in production — dev still prints the full stack, prod prints only a concise error.message. This matches the family quiet-in-prod policy (sibling runProcessApplication prints nothing in prod) and assumes the consumer's own runtime logging owns fatal-startup stacks. REVIEW-FLAGGED PLAUSIBLE REGRESSION: the sole current consumer, xyo-chain's xl1 server CLI (producer/api/finalizer/bridge), previously got a prod stack on stderr for fatal handler/actor-startup failures; if it relies on cli-kit stderr rather than its own armGlobalErrorHandling/Xl1RuntimeSession logging, prod root-cause diagnosability degrades. RESOLUTION: confirm xyo-chain's logger captures fatal startup stacks (then keep as-is + close) OR decouple in reportHandlerFailure to keep printing the raw error/stack even in prod while still dropping the usage block (default-better, no new option). Best resolved before the 3 remaining CLI migrations adopt the pattern.
title: Decide: keep quiet-in-prod handler failures, or decouple to preserve the stack trace
verification: Decision: decouple — always print full handler error (incl. stack) on stderr; still omit usage block. Changed reportHandlerFailure in packages/cli-kit-yargs/src/runYargsApplication.ts; removed toConciseMessage; updated README + runYargsApplication.spec.ts. Verified: pnpm xy test packages/cli-kit-yargs/src/spec/runYargsApplication.spec.ts — 16 passed.
